title: acciddasuite
text: acciddasuite package provides comprehensive tools, models, information building infectious disease forecasts using either national state-level data. forecasting suite focused using time series data (surveillance data) public health use create ensemble outputs statistical models, evaluation visualization tools. series vignettes demonstrate standard frameworks create evaluate forecasts using R. forecasting suite initially set run within Fable framework, tidyverse-based alternative time series forecasting R. aim demonstrate basic steps forecasting task, defined Hyndman & Athanasopoulos (2021). Updated forecasting package information can found .

title: acciddasuite
text: Ideally, load data . demonstration purposes, load data CDC National Health Safety Network. data dictionary available .
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(pipetime) library(acciddasuite) df <- get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_values = "ny") summary(df) #>      as_of              location            target          #>  Min.   :2026-01-25   Length:286         Length:286         #>  1st Qu.:2026-01-25   Class :character   Class :character   #>  Median :2026-01-25   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   #>  Mean   :2026-01-25                                         #>  3rd Qu.:2026-01-25                                         #>  Max.   :2026-01-25                                         #>  target_end_date       observation      #>  Min.   :2020-08-08   Min.   :   60.0   #>  1st Qu.:2021-12-19   1st Qu.:  461.2   #>  Median :2023-05-02   Median :  995.0   #>  Mean   :2023-05-02   Mean   : 1644.0   #>  3rd Qu.:2024-09-12   3rd Qu.: 2241.8   #>  Max.   :2026-01-24   Max.   :11833.0

title: acciddasuite
text: evaluate predictive performance, employ time series cross-validation. fit models using data available specific cutoff point (eval_start_date), forecast h weeks ahead expanding windows. want eval_start_date far back time can computationally expensive. visualise data decide eval_start_date.  Default models :  Naïve (Random Walk RW): baseline model carrying forward last observation.ETS: Exponential Smoothing state space model (automatically selected). ARIMA: Auto-Regressive Integrated Moving Average model (automatically selected).
code: # We ony evaluate on the last 30 days of data for demonstration purposes eval_start_date <- max(df$target_end_date) - 30  df |>   ggplot(aes(x = target_end_date, y = observation)) +   geom_line() +   geom_vline(     xintercept = eval_start_date,     linetype = "dashed",     color = "red"   ) +   annotate(     "label",     x = eval_start_date,     y = max(df$observation) * 0.8,     label = "Model evaluation\nstarts here",     color = "red"   ) +   scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b\n%y", breaks = "5 months") +   theme_classic() fcast = get_fcast(   df,   eval_start_date = eval_start_date,   top_n = 4, # Select top 4 models   h = 4 # forecast 4 weeks ahead ) |>   time_pipe("forecasting") #> Time Series Cross Validation... #> Forecast Generation...  fcast #> <accida_cast> #>  #> Models evaluated: #>  model_id       wis #>    <char>     <num> #>     ARIMA  39.46377 #>       ETS  39.50977 #>     THETA  43.41059 #>  ENSEMBLE  63.82600 #>    SNAIVE 272.68963 #>  #> Forecast horizon: #>   From: 2025-12-27  #>   To:   2026-02-21  #>  #> Contents: #>   $hubcast   hub forecast object #>   $score     model ranking table #>   $plot      ggplot2 object fcast$plot fcast$hubcast #> $model_out_tbl #> # A tibble: 200 × 9 #>    model_id reference_date target   horizon location target_end_date output_type #>    <chr>    <date>         <chr>      <int> <chr>    <date>          <chr>       #>  1 ARIMA    2025-12-20     wk inc …       1 NY       2025-12-27      quantile    #>  2 ARIMA    2025-12-20     wk inc …       1 NY       2025-12-27      quantile    #>  3 ARIMA    2025-12-20     wk inc …       1 NY       2025-12-27      quantile    #>  4 ARIMA    2025-12-20     wk inc …       1 NY       2025-12-27      quantile    #>  5 ARIMA    2025-12-20     wk inc …       1 NY       2025-12-27      quantile    #>  6 ARIMA    2025-12-20     wk inc …       2 NY       2026-01-03      quantile    #>  7 ARIMA    2025-12-20     wk inc …       2 NY       2026-01-03      quantile    #>  8 ARIMA    2025-12-20     wk inc …       2 NY       2026-01-03      quantile    #>  9 ARIMA    2025-12-20     wk inc …       2 NY       2026-01-03      quantile    #> 10 ARIMA    2025-12-20     wk inc …       2 NY       2026-01-03      quantile    #> # ℹ 190 more rows #> # ℹ 2 more variables: output_type_id <chr>, value <dbl> #>  #> $oracle_output #> # A tibble: 286 × 6 #>    location target_end_date target       output_type output_type_id oracle_value #>    <chr>    <date>          <chr>        <chr>       <lgl>                 <dbl> #>  1 NY       2020-08-08      wk inc covi… quantile    NA                      517 #>  2 NY       2020-08-15      wk inc covi… quantile    NA                      490 #>  3 NY       2020-08-22      wk inc covi… quantile    NA                      844 #>  4 NY       2020-08-29      wk inc covi… quantile    NA                      483 #>  5 NY       2020-09-05      wk inc covi… quantile    NA                      479 #>  6 NY       2020-09-12      wk inc covi… quantile    NA                      573 #>  7 NY       2020-09-19      wk inc covi… quantile    NA                      578 #>  8 NY       2020-09-26      wk inc covi… quantile    NA                      656 #>  9 NY       2020-10-03      wk inc covi… quantile    NA                      851 #> 10 NY       2020-10-10      wk inc covi… quantile    NA                      840 #> # ℹ 276 more rows fcast$score #> Key: <model_id> #>    model_id       wis interval_coverage_50 interval_coverage_95 #>      <char>     <num>                <num>                <num> #> 1:    ARIMA  39.46377                 0.75                    1 #> 2:      ETS  39.50977                 1.00                    1 #> 3:    THETA  43.41059                 0.50                    1 #> 4: ENSEMBLE  63.82600                 1.00                    1 #> 5:   SNAIVE 272.68963                 0.25                    1 #>    wis_relative_skill #>                 <num> #> 1:          0.6052929 #> 2:          0.6059985 #> 3:          0.6658289 #> 4:          0.9789593 #> 5:          4.1824967

title: acciddasuite
text: Additonal models can added forecasting suite defining list passing get_fcast(). models compatible fable framework (see fable documentation information).
code: library(fable) library(fable.prophet) extra <- list(   CUSTOM_ARIMA = ARIMA(observation ~ pdq(1,1,0)),   PROPHET = prophet(observation ~ season("year")),   EPIESTIM = EPIESTIM(observation, mean_si = 3, std_si = 2, rt_window = 7) )  fcast = get_fcast(   df,   eval_start_date = eval_start_date,   top_n = 4, # Select top 4 models   h = 3, # forecast 3 weeks ahead,   extra_models = extra ) |>   time_pipe("forecasting") #> Time Series Cross Validation... #> Forecast Generation...  fcast #> <accida_cast> #>  #> Models evaluated: #>      model_id        wis #>        <char>      <num> #>      EPIESTIM   30.78158 #>           ETS   32.52275 #>      ENSEMBLE   35.58608 #>         ARIMA   37.37021 #>         THETA   47.36624 #>  CUSTOM_ARIMA  126.93577 #>        SNAIVE  268.02628 #>       PROPHET 1664.29545 #>  #> Forecast horizon: #>   From: 2025-12-27  #>   To:   2026-02-14  #>  #> Contents: #>   $hubcast   hub forecast object #>   $score     model ranking table #>   $plot      ggplot2 object

title: acciddasuite
text: RespiLens platform sharing visualizing respiratory disease forecasts. submit forecasts RespiLens, can use to_respilens() jsonlite::write_json() save JSON file required format. can upload file MyRespiLens .
code: library(jsonlite) to_respilens(fcast) |>   jsonlite::write_json("respilens.json", pretty = TRUE, auto_unbox = TRUE)

title: Disease Forecast Planning for Public Health
text: Disease forecasting important tool public health, however, maximize utility tools, thoughtful planning required clearly define approach problem needing addressed. ACCIDDA Forecast Suite provides comprehensive toolkit building infectious disease forecasting pipelines hubs, discussions planning prior starting forecasting project important. provide series questions consider addressing prior using package. package suite still active development, welcome contributions feedback community.

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: Estimates current reproduction number (Rt) using EpiEstim simulates future incidence using projections. Works regular aggregation period integrates fable workflow via model().

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
code: EPIESTIM(   formula,   mean_si,   std_si,   rt_window = 14L,   n_sim = 100L,   R_fix_within = TRUE )

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: formula Response variable, e.g. observation. Exogenous regressors supported. mean_si Mean serial interval days. std_si Standard deviation serial interval days. rt_window Sliding window width days Rt estimation (dt_out EpiEstim). Controls many recent days inform current Rt. Smaller values track recent trends closely; larger values give smoother estimate. Defaults 14 days (~2 weeks). n_sim Number simulation paths forecast distribution. R_fix_within TRUE, Rt held constant within simulated path (recommended short horizons).

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: model definition use inside model().

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: aggregation period detected automatically tsibble index. recent data (rt_window estimation period plus serial interval period) passed EpiEstim's expectation-maximisation (EM) algorithm, reconstructs daily incidence aggregated counts estimating Rt rolling rt_window-day windows. recent Rt estimate used project forward via stochastic simulation. Forecasts returned sample distributions (one per horizon period) drawn n_sim epidemic paths.

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
text: Fetch recent confirmed US hospital admissions NHSN source COVID-19, influenza, RSV using pub_covidcast.

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
text: pathogen Character. One "covid", "flu" "rsv". geo_values Character vector. Geographic values per pub_covidcast filter . Defaults "*" (states).

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
text: tibble hubverse format columns: as_of: Date data issued location: State code target: Target description (e.g., "wk inc covid hosp") target_end_date: End date target week observation: Observed hospital admissions

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
code: get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_values = c("ca", "ny")) #> # A tibble: 572 × 5 #>    as_of      location target            target_end_date observation #>    <date>     <chr>    <chr>             <date>                <dbl> #>  1 2026-01-25 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08             4836 #>  2 2026-01-25 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #>  3 2026-01-25 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-15             4273 #>  4 2026-01-25 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-15              490 #>  5 2026-01-25 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-22             3498 #>  6 2026-01-25 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-22              844 #>  7 2026-01-25 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-29             3100 #>  8 2026-01-25 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-29              483 #>  9 2026-01-25 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-09-05             2888 #> 10 2026-01-25 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-09-05              479 #> # ℹ 562 more rows

what: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
text: Fits multiple time series models weekly incidence data evaluates short term predictive performance using expanding window rolling origin scheme.

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
code: get_fcast(df, eval_start_date, h = 4, top_n = 3, extra_models = NULL)

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
text: df Data frame weekly observations containing target_end_date (Date) observation (numeric). eval_start_date Date string coercible Date. First date forecasts evaluated. least 52 weeks data must precede date. h Integer. Forecast horizon weeks. Default 4. top_n Integer. Number top ranked models included ensemble. Default 3. extra_models Named list additional forecasting models passed fabletools::model(). element must reference observation column compatible fabletools::forecast(). Custom models can constructed using fable modelling framework, see fabletools extension_models vignette. name list element used model label output.

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
text: object class accida_cast containing: forecast Final h week ahead forecasts models ensemble. score Model ranking based rolling origin WIS. plot ggplot object showing forecasts prediction intervals.

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
text: eval_start_date onwards, models repeatedly refitted data available evaluation time point used forecast next h weeks. Models ranked mean WIS score across evaluation periods. best performing top_n models combined equal weight ensemble. final h week ahead forecast produced refitting selected models using full dataset.

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ extra_models <- list(   CUSTOM_ARIMA = ARIMA(observation ~ pdq(1, 1, 0)),   PROPHET = fable.prophet::PROPHET(observation),   EPIESTIM = EPIESTIM(observation, mean_si = 3.96, std_si = 4.75) )  get_fcast(   df,   eval_start_date = "2025-01-01",   h = 4,   top_n = 3,   extra_models = extra_models ) } # }
